mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) signal a recovery in residential construction, a leading indicator of economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following an increase in HOUST are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.1) or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points signals a turnaround in the labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as employment improves.
failure_condition: Triggered instances show negative average 20-day returns or a win rate below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate that accommodative monetary policy is effectively stimulating consumer demand, a strong positive signal for S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal derived from FEDFUNDS and RSAFS has a Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window during the zero-interest-rate regime.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, increasing industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side normalization and economic strengthening, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: During the post-recession recovery regime, the signal produces average 20-day returns indistinguishable from zero or Rank IC below 0.025.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, a direct driver of corporate revenues and short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signals produce negative median 5-day returns or a win rate below 50% in walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 0.3% indicates emerging inflation due to recovering demand, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns in a post-deflation environment.
failure_condition: Triggered events show negative average 20-day returns or subsequent CPI declines within the next quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in both payroll employment (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signal a broad-based economic recovery, strongly predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The composite signal (e.g., standardized sum of PAYEMS and INDPRO changes) has a Rank IC below 0.02 over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a deep recession regime, a positive quarterly change in real GDP (GDPC1) signals the trough of the business cycle and the onset of recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: During deep recession periods, positive GDP surprises do not result in positive average 60-day forward returns or the win rate is below 50%.
